Plaintiffs Motion For Clarification And Or Reconsideration Of The February 10, 2023 Court Order Adopting The Discovery Referee'S Report And Recommended Order No. 9
Off Calendar Apr-17-2023 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF OMONDI NYONG'O's Motion For Clarification And Or Reconsideration Of The February 10, 2023 Court Order Adopting The Discovery Referee'S Report And Recommended Order No. 9

No appearance.

The court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF OMONDI NYONG'O's Motion For Clarification And Or Reconsideration Of The February 10, 2023 Court Order Adopting The Discovery Referee'S Report And Recommended Order No. 9 as follows:

"Plaintiff's motion for clarification and/or reconsideration of the February 10, 2023 court order adopting the discovery referee's report and recommended order no. 9" is denied.
A reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) The moving party shall state by affidavit "what new or different facts, circumstances, or law are claimed to be shown." (CCP 1008.) Plaintiff does not satisfy these requirements.

"The name of the motion is not controlling, and, regardless of the name, a motion asking the trial court to decide the same matter previously ruled on is a motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008." (Powell v. County of Orange (2011) 197 Cal.App.4th 1573, 1577.)

Plaintiff's objections were considered.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH, PALO ALTO MEDICAL FOUNDATION'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT.

Sutter Health and Palo Alto Medical Foundation's (PAMF) demurrer to the second amended complaint (SAC) is overruled.

The SAC pleads that Sutter Health is a nonprofit corporation and that the two other defendants - PAMF and Palo Alto Foundation Medical Group - are "[u]nder its control" and "its affiliates." (Id. at 3:4-6.) Further, the SAC pleads that the three defendants are plaintiff's joint employers, exercising "joint control over" his "wages, hours and working conditions." (Id. at 3:14-17.)

Defendants argue that the SAC factually errs because they are "three separate and distinct entities" and not plaintiff's joint employers. (Memo. 5:25, 7:2-3.) Defendants may be right or may be wrong, but this is not for resolution at the pleading stage, when a plaintiff's allegations must be accepted as true. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) The summary judgment case defendants rely on - Laird v. Capital Cities/ABC, Inc. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 727 - is not to the contrary. (See Memo. 7:3-4.)

The SAC adequately pleads its claims. It is notable that defendants have twice answered the same joint allegations before. (Opp. 5:1-8.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DANIEL M. HUTCHINSON, Esq. of Lieff Cabraser Heimann & Bernstein, LLP (Tel: 415-956-1000) for plaintiff;
Atty. ACEILYA G. BURTON, Esq. of Fox Rothschild LLP (Tel: 415-364-5540) for defendants Sutter Health and Palo Alto Medical Foundation;
Atty. AYUSHI NEOGI, Esq. of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP (Tel: 415-986-5900) for defendant Palo Alto Foundation Medical Group.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF OMONDI NYONG'O's Motion For Clarification And-Or Reconsideration.

Plaintiff's "motion for clarification and reconsideration" of this court's October 5, 2022 order is denied. That order approved and made an order of the court a discovery referee report by the Hon. Jamie Jacobs-May (ret.). No hearing was required. (Marathon Nat. Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 1256, 1258.)

A reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law" and a valid reason for not presenting them earlier. (CCP SEC 1008; Gilberd v. AC Transit (1995) 32 Cal.App.4th 1494, 1500.) Plaintiff makes none of these showings. Rather, Judge Jacobs-May's 21-page report makes clear that the objections made again in the pending motion were already presented.

To the extent clarification is deemed necessary, the court has again reviewed Judge Jacobs-May's report as well as plaintiff's objections thereto and again adopts the judge's report and recommendations.

Order signed.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT PALO ALTO FOUNDATION MEDICAL GROUP's Motion For A Discovery Referee.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Argument presented by counsel for parties. Matter passed to be re-called at 1:30 p.m.

Matter re-called at 1:30 p.m. Further argument presented by counsel for parties. Matter submitted.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification, as follows:

The Pro Tem discloses that he has treating doctors at Sutter Health in San Francisco. This does not affect his ability to decide this motion fairly on the merits and is not grounds for disqualification.

The motion is granted. It is necessary to appoint a referee to hear and determine any and all discovery motions and disputes relevant to discovery in the action and to report findings and make a recommendation thereon. The referee is appointed for all discovery purposes. The reason is that discovery in this case has been unduly contentious and slow, including without limitation in the scheduling of depositions and the production of documents, and there have been multiple discovery motions. The appointment of a single referee to resolve all discovery disputes should result in discovery disputes being resolved more quickly and will probably reduce the number of discovery issues which the parties dispute. The referee's fees shall be paid 1/2 by PAFMG, and 1/2 by Sutter Health and Sutter Bay Medical Foundation.

By June 14, 2022, the parties shall either file with the Court a joint document stating who they have agreed to as a discovery referee, or else, plaintiff, PAFMG and Sutter may each file with the Court a list of up to three persons who they propose to be the referee and include all information required under the statute. Courtesy copies of these documents shall be emailed to the pro tem at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com.

In addition to submitting their discovery motion papers to the referee, the parties will also file them with the court so that there is a public record in this discrimination case.

Prevailing party to prepare and submit a proposed order to the pro tem judge at bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com. The Court with hold off on signing a proposed order until the discovery referee is appointed.

Hearing Re: Plaintiff's Motion To Quash.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications as follows:

The motion is DENIED IN PART, GRANTED IN PART. While the subpoenas are directed to appropriate providers and include appropriate subject-matter for discovery, the subpoenas are overly broad as written. Each subpoena is to be modified to delete reference to "any" record and only to seek records reflecting the dates of service and symptoms or conditions disclosed in response to interrogatories. Additionally, the plaintiff's request for a "first look" at all documents produced is GRANTED. Defendant to instruct the subpoena service to produce records only to plaintiff's counsel. Plaintiff is to provide immediate notice to defendants of each production plaintiff receives from the subpoena service. Plaintiff's counsel to review the records for discoverability. Plaintiff's counsel to preserve the original production "as is." After review, should plaintiff's counsel conclude that any record, or portion thereof, should be either excluded or redacted, counsel shall do so and produce the remaining documents along with a detailed privilege log within 10 days of receipt of the final batch of documents from the subpoena service. Should defendant's counsel consider any of the items on the privilege log improperly withheld, the parties shall meet and confer in good faith to attempt to resolve any issues within 10 days thereafter. If any disputed items remain, the parties shall jointly contact the Judge Pro Tem (noah@ndllegal.com) and arrange for an in camera inspection of the withheld records.

Court to prepare a formal order.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Mary Ann Scanlan, CSR No. 8875, Tel: 415-834-1114, Email: ma@scanlanstone.com (302/JPT)
